prompt_inputs.goal: Strength and muscle gain for basketball season

solution_criteria: Plan is compact (5-6 meals max) and fits on a single page
Macronutrient ratios align with strength training (1.6-2.2g protein per kg bodyweight)
Pre- and post-workout meals are strategically timed within 1-2 hours of training
Total daily calories support muscle gain (maintenance + 300-500 surplus)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a standard strength-training athlete (basketball player) with no restrictions but specific macronutrient needs to validate that the compact plan prioritizes nutrient timing around workouts and maintains proper macro ratios

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains 4-5 eating occasions spread throughout the day
Total daily protein is 90-110g (1.6-2.0g per kg body weight)
Total daily calories are 2200-2400 (40-44 kcal per kg for muscle gain without excess bulk)
Meals are described as light, nutrient-dense, and avoid heavy/bloating foods unsuitable for climbing
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a lightweight athlete in a weight-sensitive sport (rock climber, 160cm, 55kg) with a muscle-building goal to ensure the meal plan balances sufficient calories and protein without excessive volume or heaviness

prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training - high-performance endurance athlete requiring 3000+ calories daily with optimal carb-to-protein ratio for recovery

solution_criteria: Meal plan includes minimum 3 vegan protein sources (legumes, tofu, nuts, seeds) meeting ~90g daily protein requirement
All meals and snacks are explicitly gluten-free with no cross-contamination risks
Caloric density supports 3000+ calorie intake across 4-5 meals/snacks in compact format
Carbohydrate-rich options included for endurance fuel with digestible, low-fiber pre-run meals
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-performance endurance athlete (marathon runner) with multiple dietary restrictions (vegan and gluten-free) to verify the meal plan accounts for protein sources, caloric density, and digestibility within compact format
